返回本批次

美国声誉跌至“本世纪未见之深”且“可能永远无法恢复”

US reputation hits 'depths not seen this century' – and 'may never recover'

www.rawstory.com 作者 doener 分数 53 评论 39 2026/04/07 07:04
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 2026 年 3 月 28 日，美国华盛顿特区，在全国范围内反对美国总统唐纳德·特朗普政府政策的示威活动中，一名示威者在林肯纪念堂前举行“禁止国王”抗议活动，举着倒置的美国国旗。路透社/伊芙琳·霍克斯坦 TPX 当天图片 美国对伊朗战争正在进行，这场战争于 2 月底开始，造成 100 多名伊朗人死亡一位前奥巴马政府公共外交负责人周日警告说，“我相信，由于唐纳德·特朗普总统和他在伊朗的战争，美国的受欢迎程度将下降到本世纪从未见过的深度，并且可能永远无法恢复到我们在吉米·卡特和罗纳德·里根时期看到的中位水平。” 《卫报》：“忘掉巴拉克·奥巴马的数字吧——它们遥不可及。在入侵伊朗之前，人们对特朗普驾驭全球事务能力的信心已经达到了 30-40% 左右。这将是新的上限。”斯坦格尔将他的前工作描述为美国的“‘美国品牌’首席营销官”，他的主要任务之一是“帮助塑造和提升美国的海外形象。”然而，随着特朗普发动了一场针对伊朗的战争，这场战争受到了伊朗的谴责。斯坦格尔警告说，美国的盟友是非法的，美国通过在海外培养文化影响力来投射“软实力”的能力可能终于结束了，这在很大程度上要归功于特朗普。“自伍德罗·威尔逊以来，美国总统一直从事民主促进事业。那个时代现在可能已经结束了，”他写道。“随着对伊朗的战争、美国在委内瑞拉的行动以及对古巴的日益好战的谈论，特朗普政府正在复兴丑陋的美国人的老比喻，但这一次没有曾经对民主必须歌颂的美国作为一个自恋且对文化不敏感的恶霸的旧形象卷土重来。

Original Summary

  1. A demonstrator holds an upside-down American flag during a "No Kings" protest in front of the Lincoln Memorial, amid nationwide demonstrations against U.S. President Donald Trump's administration policies, in Washington, D.C., U.S., March 28, 2026. REUTERS/Evelyn Hockstein TPX IMAGES OF THE DAY The ongoing U.S. war against Iran, which kicked off in late February with the killing of more than 100 Iranian schoolchildren, has undermined a key State Department foreign policy tool and may have irreparably damaged the nation’s global reputation, a former Obama administration public diplomacy chief warned Sunday.“I believe because of [President] Donald Trump and his war in Iran, American popularity will descend to depths it has not seen this century and may never recover to the median levels that we saw with Jimmy Carter and Ronald Reagan,” wrote Richard Stengel, former State Department under secretary for diplomacy, in an analysis published Sunday in The Guardian.“Forget Barack Obama numbers – they’re out of reach. Confidence in Trump’s ability to navigate global affairs was already around 30-40% before the invasion of Iran. That will be the new ceiling.”Stengel described his former job as being the United States’ “chief marketing officer of ‘Brand USA,’” with among his chief missions being to “help shape and promote America’s image abroad.”With Trump having launched a war against Iran, however, one that has been condemned by America’s allies as unlawful, the United States’ ability to project “soft power” through fostering cultural influence abroad may have finally come to an end, Stengel warned, and largely due to Trump.“Since Woodrow Wilson, American presidents have been in the democracy promotion business. That era may now be over,” he wrote.“With the war on Iran, US action in Venezuela, and increasingly bellicose talk about Cuba, the Trump administration is reviving the old trope of the Ugly American, but this time without the once obligatory paeans to democracy. That old image of America as a narcissistic and culturally insensitive bully is back with a vengeance.”

评论（默认中文）

dtagames 2026/04/07 07:41
我们还揭开了我们是不可阻挡的军事强国这一神话的面纱。我们看起来很愚蠢，日复一日地说战争已经结束，他们无能为力等等，而同一个频道却表明这不是真的。

目前印象最差的是中国和俄罗斯，它们肯定会看到一个新的地区大国体系在自己的领域内运作，而不是一个显然是历史虚构的单一全球大国。

优秀的书， 文明的冲突 预测了这种向地区大国的转变，与 50 年代简单的东西方分歧以及我们现在看到的许多其他当前事件相对应。这是30年前写的。

We've also lifted the veil on the myth that we were a unstoppable military power. We look silly saying, day after day, that the war is over, they're powerless, etc while the same channel shows that's not true.

The folks least impressed right now are China and Russia, who must surely see a new system of regional powers operating in their own spheres, not a single global power which is apparently a historical fiction.

The excellent book, Clash of Civilizations predicted this move to regional powers versus the 50's simple East/West divide, along with many other current events we see now. It was written 30 years ago.

在 HN 中查看
stvltvs 2026/04/07 08:59
美国在没有盟友的情况下相对较弱。北约才是西方真正的超级大国。现任政权自暴自弃，试图单干。
The US is relatively weak without its allies. NATO was the real superpower in the west. The current regime got too big for their britches and tried to go it alone.
在 HN 中查看
SanjayMehta 2026/04/07 10:28
多年来情况并非如此。

除美国外，所有其他国家的现役军人总数约为 200 万人。其中土耳其最大，有300k。

由于语言问题、文化问题和政治问题，他们不太可能走到一起，尤其是现在，特朗普和万斯公开侮辱他们的北约伙伴。

今天，北约能够为美国提供的唯一筹码是政治筹码。

不要忘记，过去四年来，俄罗斯通过乌克兰安全情报办公室一直在系统性地使整个北约非军事化。德国等北约附庸国的惊慌失措的声明就是证据。最近，波兰拒绝向伊朗冲突派遣其爱国者系统。

That hasn't been true for many years.

Excluding the US, all the other countries put together would be around 2 million active personnel. Of which Turkey is the largest at 300k.

Due to language issues, cultural issues, and political issues they're hardly likely to pull together, and especially now, with Trump and Vance openly insulting their NATO partners.

The only leverage NATO would be able to provide the US today would be political.

And don't forget that Russia via the Ukraine SMO has been systematically demilitarising the entirety of NATO over the last 4 years. The evidence for this is in the panicked pronouncements of the NATO vassal states like Germany. More recently Poland has refused to send their patriot systems to the Iran conflict.

flowerthoughts 2026/04/07 13:49
自 2022 年 2 月以来，莱茵金属公司的股价上涨了 1,439%，萨博公司的股价上涨了 991%。欧洲国防材料公司的股价上涨得很好。
Rheinmetall stock is up 1,439% and Saab is up 991% since February 2022. European defense materials companies are ramping up just fine.
在 HN 中查看
bulbar 2026/04/07 14:08
> 今天，北约能够为美国提供的唯一筹码是政治筹码。

不确定你在说什么。北约允许美国在其国家设立军事基地，为伊朗战争提供了关键支持。

> The only leverage NATO would be able to provide the US today would be political.

Not sure what you are saying. NATO provides critical support for the Iran war by allowing the US having military bases in their countries.

在 HN 中查看
smitty1e 2026/04/07 10:19
> 目前印象最差的是中国和俄罗斯

在委内瑞拉行动成功后；库姆的斩首罢工；然后是这次救援任务：另一种可能性是，莫斯科和北京正在悄悄地重新评估他们希望在华盛顿安装另一个拜登式的肉傀儡之前有多好战。

> The folks least impressed right now are China and Russia

After a successful Venezuela op; the decapitation strike in Qom; and then this rescue mission: another possibility is that Moscow and Beijing are quietly reassessing just how belligerent they want to be ahead of installing another Biden-style meat puppet in DC.

在 HN 中查看
bulbar 2026/04/07 14:14
北京并不关心行动的结果，他们关心的是大局。特朗普将西方一分为二，这对中国来说是件好事。 此外，每一种孤立主义政治都对他们有利，因为他们试图入侵台湾，而美国变得越孤立，他们实际尝试时的抵抗就会越少。

对于俄罗斯来说，美国袭击伊朗真是太好了，因为世界需要石油，而现在俄罗斯石油又回到了谈判桌上。另一方面，美国愿意向乌克兰提供的武器数量以及继续支持防御俄罗斯帝国主义和夺取权力的政治意愿都在下降。 一切以任何方式削弱北约的事情对俄罗斯来说都是好事，因此他们对特朗普威胁该联盟感到非常高兴。

Bejing doesn't care for results of op, they care for the big picture. Trump is splitting the West in half, which is great for China. Also every piece of isolationist politics is good for them, because they try to invade Taiwan and the more isolationist the US becomes, the less resistant will there be when they actually try.

For Russia, it's just great the US attacked Iran, because the world needs Oil and now Russian oil is back on the table. The amount of weapons that the US is willed to deliver to the Ukrain on the other hand goes down as well as the political will to continue the support of the defense against Russian imperialism and grab for power. Everything that weakens NATO in any way is great for Russia, so they are quite happy with Trump threatening the alliance.

在 HN 中查看
orwin 2026/04/07 15:36
我认为中国领导层认为不需要入侵台湾。与一个高度专业化的国家竞争是很困难的，但如果他们能够生产出与台湾一样好 90% 的芯片，那么台湾将迎来一场疯狂的经济之旅，最终台湾可能会成为一个不开枪的中国自治区。
I don't think China leadership thinks it needs to invade Taiwan. Competing against an hyper-specialized country is hard, but if they manage to produce chips 90% as good as Taiwan, the later is in for a wild economic ride, which will probably end up as Taiwan as an autonomous Chinese region without firing any guns.
在 HN 中查看
Markoff 2026/04/07 15:49
确切地说，有些人相信中国入侵台湾的说法是非常天真的，看看香港发生的事情，它的规模相当于中国经济的 30%（当他们接管时），这就是为什么他们同意自治，现在它下降到了 - 3%？基本上与普通的中国城市发展无关，台湾也会发生同样的情况，人们最终会用钱包投票，特别是如果另一个选择是来自疯狂统治的美国老年政府的支持

认为中国需要入侵台湾的人在西方5年的选举周期中是非常短视的想法，这在中国行不通，与这些西方营销产品不同，中国在世界上有足够的时间等待他们所谓的政客1-2个任期（我写这并不是对中国的钦佩，只是陈述事实，无论你喜欢与否，他们的政治制度以不同的方式运作，但我肯定更喜欢欧盟与中国而不是美国在安全方面达成协议，他们更可靠，而不是虐待伙伴）

exactly, it's very naive some folks fall for this China invading Taiwan narrative, just look what happened with Hong Kong, it had size of like 30% of Chinese economy (when they took over), which is why they agreed with autonomy, now it's down to like what - 3%? basically irelevant ordinary develoepd CN city, same will happen with Taiwan, people will vote with their wallets in the end especially if the other option is support from US gerontocracy ruled by crazies

people thinking China needs to invade Taiwan are very short-sighted thinking in western 5 year election cycles, which don't work in China, China has all the time in the world to wait unlike these western marketing products for 1-2 terms they call politicians (and I don't write it as some China admiration, just stating the facts their political system works different way whether you like it or not, but I would for sure prefer EU making deals with China instead of US also regarding security, they are more reliable not abusive partner)

KellyCriterion 2026/04/07 15:40
> 世界需要石油，现在俄罗斯石油又回到人们的餐桌上

上周，俄罗斯宣布将立即停止任何石油出口

> the world needs Oil and now Russian oil is back on the tabl

Last week, Russia announced that any export of Oil will be stopped immediately

在 HN 中查看
f30e3dfed1c9 2026/04/07 09:18
许多美国人还没有明白这一点，但我们正在经历的是1945年开始的有时被称为“战后国际秩序”的终结。美国在西欧的盟友被故意疏远，我们的选民也表现出太不稳定、不可预测和愚蠢，无法选出一个值得信赖的政府。

我们的盟友曾经（名义上仍然是）的情报共享受到了限制，因为没有人相信与我们共享的信息不会传到其他不希望它们好的国家。如果确实可以的话，这种信任将需要几十年的时间才能重建，到那时，世界将变得非常不同。

现任政府受到宗教狂热分子的控制，他们对世界抱有妄想、世界末日的观点，他们来自的政党中的大部分人也是如此。明智的人不会也不应该相信这样的人。需要一代人的时间才能将这些人从政治权力中赶走，而且目前还不清楚大多数选民是否愿意这样做。

与此同时，美国正在摧毁理应令全世界羡慕的科学和教育基础设施，并让自己对几乎全世界的移民怀有敌意，而长期以来，吸引最优秀和最聪明的人才对它来说却大有裨益。同样，几年内造成的损害将需要几十年才能恢复。

现在还不是收拾行李的时候，但现在是时候认识到，美国现在已经不再、而且很可能永远不会再拥有 1945 年至 2017 年那样的世界地位了。现在大多数成年人长大的美国已经不复存在，而他们的子孙将居住的美国可能会大大缩小。

这本来不必发生，但这就是我们现在的情况，而且是我们自己造成的。

Lots of Americans don't get it yet but what we're living through is the end of what was sometimes called the "post-war international order" that began in 1945. America's allies in western Europe have been deliberately alienated and our electorate has shown itself to be too volatile, unpredictable, and frankly dumb to elect a trustworthy government.

Intelligence-sharing from countries once, and still sort of nominally, our allies has been curtailed because no one can trust that information shared with us won't make its way to other countries that do not wish them well. That trust will take decades to rebuild if in fact it can be, and by that time, the world will be a very different place.

The current administration is in the grip of religious fanatics with delusional, apocalyptic views of the world, as is much of the political party they come from. Nobody sensible trusts people like that, nor should they. It will take a generation to remove these people from political power, and it's far from clear that a majority of the electorate even wants to.

Meanwhile, the US is gutting the science and education infrastructure that was rightly the envy of the world and making itself hostile to immigrants from nearly the entire world, when being a draw to the best and brightest served it so well for so long. Again, damage being done in a matter of years will take decades to recover from.

It's not time to pack it in but it is time to recognize that America does not now and will in all likelihood never again hold the place in the world it did from 1945 to 2017. The America that most adults alive now grew up in is gone and the one their children and grandchildren will inhabit will likely be much diminished.

Didn't have to happen but that's where we are and we brought it on ourselves.

在 HN 中查看
bulbar 2026/04/07 14:30
我完全同意，这是非常明显的，甚至是很大一部分美国人希望通过投票支持“美国优先”。

我希望欧盟能够填补权力真空。他们已经在超快地形成新的经济纽带并启动新的防御计划。但它需要一支真正的欧盟军队。还需要一种新的运作模式，纯粹基于共识的模式效果不太好。

I fully agree, it's super obvious and even wanted by a huge share of the US population by voting for "America first".

I hope that the EU manages to fill the power vacuum. They are already forming new economic bonds super fast and starting new defense initiatives. It will need a real EU military though. Also a new mode to operate is needed, the pure consensus based mode doesn't work that well.

在 HN 中查看
lunar-whitey 2026/04/07 12:57
美国目前的处境与1956年英国在苏伊士运河的处境相似，并且正在冲刺到1979年苏联在阿富汗的处境。然而，观看网络电视新闻或阅读《纽约时报》的美国人很难将其与2003年的伊拉克区分开来。
The U.S. is currently in a similar position to the U.K. in the Suez in 1956 and is sprinting into the position the U.S.S.R. was in Afghanistan in 1979. Americans watching network TV news or reading the New York Times, however, would be hard pressed to distinguish it from Iraq in 2003.

Trump destroyed very much of the softpower procellaine the US had built over the last decades.

在 HN 中查看
SanjayMehta 2026/04/07 10:34
悲歌的主人公竟然认为美国曾经被认为是仁慈的，这是可笑的。

这就是美国政客和官僚的问题。

他们没有受过教育，没有文化知识，缺乏了解对方的能力和愿望。他们总是表现得好像他们不必或将自己的恶意意图投射到别人身上。

我们应该赞扬特朗普撕下了美国“外交”的薄薄面具。

The fact that the protagonist in this lament ever thought that the US was even once considered benevolent is risible.

That's the problem with USAian politicians and bureaucrats.

They have no education, no cultural knowledge, and lack the ability and the desire to understand the other side. They always act as if they don't have to OR project their own malicious intentions onto others.

We should give credit to Trump for ripping off the thin mask of US "diplomacy."

在 HN 中查看
jmclnx 2026/04/07 07:27
我认为，如果共和党在 2026 年损失惨重，以至于特朗普可以被弹劾并被免职，并且他的爪牙因腐败而被定罪，我认为它将会恢复。 我相信世界正在等待 2026 年 11 月，然后再做出重大改变。

如果这没有发生，我会说这篇文章 100% 真实。

I think if the GOP looses big in 2026 to the point Trump can be impeached and removed from office and his minions are convicted for corruption, I think it will recover. I believe the world is waiting for Nov 2026 before making big changes.

If that does not happen, I would say the article is 100% true.

在 HN 中查看
rjrjrjrj 2026/04/07 07:49
这份善意第一次就用完了。 至少需要一代人的时间才能从重新选举罪犯中恢复过来。
That goodwill was used up the first time. It will take at least a generation to recover from re-electing the criminal.
在 HN 中查看
burkaman 2026/04/07 07:47
世界其他地方真的会关注这样的内部政治细节吗？我无法想象非美国人的平均想法：“我知道他们今年将举行立法选举，这可能会影响特朗普实施其议程的能力，在那之前我会保留判断。”我认为这更像是“美国无缘无故地投下炸弹并摧毁了全球经济，他们为什么要这样做”。

即使作为内部人士也很难理解一个国家如何能够再次选举地球上最糟糕的人，然后在两年后投票让反对派上台，因此很难相信外部人士会采取如此微妙的观点。

Does the rest of the world really pay attention to internal political details like that? I can't imagine the average non-American thinking "well I know they have a legislative election this year that may impact Trump's ability to enact his agenda, I'll reserve judgement until then." I assume it's more like "America is dropping bombs for no reason and destroying the global economy, why are they doing that".

Even as an insider it's hard to understand how a country could re-elect the worst person on earth and then two years later vote the opposition into power, so it's hard to believe that outsiders are taking such a nuanced view.

在 HN 中查看
HeavyStorm 2026/04/07 10:51
决策者确实关注美国的内政，因为它直接影响世界其他地区。
Decision makers do pay attention to US internal affairs as it affects the rest of the world directly.
在 HN 中查看
thefz 2026/04/07 15:04
比那更糟糕，朋友。我们得到了明显的证实，事实上，资本主义确实并且永远会为绝大多数人（除了百分之一的人口）带来最糟糕的结果。

说实话，这位总统没有因性虐待丑闻和发动一场没人想要的战争而被弹劾，这一事实充分说明了美国民众对其政府的疏离和不感兴趣。

Worse than that, friend. We have the flaring confirmation that in fact capitalism does and will always lead to the shittiest outcome for the vast majority bar a one percent of the population.

And to be honest the fact that this president is not impeached over a sex abuse scandal and starting a war nobody wanted says a lot about how detached and uninterested the population of the US is from its government.

在 HN 中查看
dragonwriter 2026/04/07 07:59
> 我认为，如果共和党在 2026 年损失惨重，以至于特朗普可以被弹劾并被免职，并且他的爪牙因腐败而被定罪，我认为它将会恢复。

假设在该问题上进行党派路线投票且没有任何一方叛逃，则要求民主党赢得待选举的 35 个参议院席位中的 33 个（如果他们占据目前拥有的所有席位，则需要他们赢得共和党占据的 22 个席位中的 20 个。）

> 我相信世界正在等待 2026 年 11 月，然后再做出重大改变。

我认为世界根本没有在等待，只是需要时间来确定重大变化的形式，无论是欧洲防务一体化取代美国的历史关键作用，还是各国正在积极、公开地致力于的任何其他大量变革。

现在，如果美国目前的方向发生变化，其中一些努力可能会被放弃或降低优先级，但“可能会停止工作”与“等待开始”并不是一回事。

> I think if the GOP looses big in 2026 to the point Trump can be impeached and removed from office and his minions are convicted for corruption, I think it will recover.

Assuming party-line voting on the issue with no defections from either party, that requires the Democrats to win 33 of the 35 Senate seats up for election (if they hold every one that they currently hold, it requires them to take 20 of the 22 Republican-held seats.)

> I believe the world is waiting for Nov 2026 before making big changes.

I don't think the world is waiting at all, it is just taking time to work out the shape of the big changes, whether its European defense integration to replace the historically-pivotal role of the US, or any of large number of other changes nations are actively and openly working on.

Now, if the present direction of the US changes, some of those efforts may be abandoned or deprioritized, but "could potentially stop work" is not the same thing as "waiting to start".

在 HN 中查看
jmclnx 2026/04/07 09:47
>假设在该问题上进行党派路线投票且没有任何一方叛逃，则需要民主党赢得 33 票

我知道，这种情况发生的可能性很小。 但我只是指出我认为这篇文章需要发生什么才算是错误的。

另一条评论中有人提到了军队。 一个失败/法西斯的美国及其军队是我真正为世界担心的事情。我认为 2026 年 11 月是美国改变道路的最后机会。

>Assuming party-line voting on the issue with no defections from either party, that requires the Democrats to win 33

I know, it is very unlikely this will happen. But I was just pointing out what I think needs to happen for the article to be wrong.

And someone in another comment brought up the military. A failing/fascist US with its military is something I really worry about for the world. I think Nov 2026 is the last chance the US has to change path.

在 HN 中查看
dawnerd 2026/04/07 07:59
其他国家都经历过非常黑暗的时期，也不是很受尊重的国家。美国应该不会有什么不同，但我们肯定会受到很大的挫折，如果中期选举没有产生任何有意义的结果，那就更是如此。
Other countries have had very dark times and are not pretty well respected nations. US shouldn't be any different, but we're definitely being set back a lot, even more so if midterms don't result in anything meaningful.
在 HN 中查看
slyall 2026/04/07 07:36
问题是，即使美国在 14 年内选出了一些优秀的中间派民主党人，每个国家都会想“如果他们选举特朗普第二次怎么办？”

我的意思是，美国在第一次选举后已经再次选举他，所以这不是一次性的。美国的盟友已经在增加国防开支并使供应链（尤其是武器）多样化，远离美国。

你会把你国家的安全押在美国未来的稳定上吗？

The problem is that even if the US elects some nice centrist democrats for the 14 years every country is going to be thinking "What if they elect Trump v2?"

I mean the US already re-elected him after the first time so it wasn't a one off. US allies are already increasing defense spending and diversifying supply chains (especially for weapons) away from the US.

Would you bet the safety of your country on the US being stable going forward?

在 HN 中查看
JumpCrisscross 2026/04/07 07:39
> 问题是，即使美国在 14 年内选出了一些优秀的中间派民主党人，每个国家都会思考“如果他们选举特朗普第二次怎么办？”

在意见页面之外实际上没有人这样做。例如，阿根廷已九次拖欠债务。它仍然找到贷款人。同样，外交政策稳定的美国仍然代表着军事超级大国和消费动力，任何理性领导人都很难不与之结盟。

> problem is that even if the US elects some nice centrist democrats for the 14 years every country is going to be thinking "What if they elect Trump v2?"

Nobody actually does this outside opinion pages. Like, Argentina has defaulted on its debt nine times. It still finds lenders. Similarly, an America that has stabilised its foreign policy still represents a military superpower and consumer dynamo that would be hard for any rational leader to pass up aligning with.

在 HN 中查看
yongjik 2026/04/07 08:01
对比：日本早在四十年代就犯下了一些战争罪行，近一个世纪后，许多东亚人仍然认为日本也许不应该拥有一支可以投射力量的正规军队。 这其中包括很大一部分日本公民本身。
Counterpoint: Japan committed some war crimes back in the 40s, and almost a century later, a lot of East Asians still think maybe Japan shouldn't have a regular military that can project its power. And that includes a large portion of Japanese citizens themselves.
在 HN 中查看
JumpCrisscross 2026/04/07 08:29
> 日本在 40 年代犯下了一些战争罪行，近一个世纪后，许多东亚人仍然认为日本可能不应该拥有一支可以投射力量的正规军队

我实际上想说这是一个不错的推论与对位。你攻击的人会恨你。不管你是否真正改变。但我们现在并没有直接攻击我们的盟友。那些没有直接受到日本战争罪行的人们， 例如 南亚进展顺利。

> Japan committed some war crimes back in the 40s, and almost a century later, a lot of East Asians still think maybe Japan shouldn't have a regular military that can project its power

I'd actually say that's a decent corollary versus counterpoint. The folks you attack will hate you. Regardless of whether you genuinely change. But we aren't directly attacking our allies right now. The folks who weren't directly war crimed by Japan, e.g. South Asia, have moved along just fine.

在 HN 中查看
d3vnull 2026/04/07 08:49
也许对盟友的攻击不是用炸弹完成的，但攻击无论如何都会发生，仇恨也在增长。

在这一切发生之前，美国并不是非常受欢迎，它更被容忍，而不是被庆祝。这根大稻草压断了骆驼的背，每个人都在努力离开，完成后为什么还要回来？

Maybe the attacks on allies aren't being done with bombs but the attacks are landing anyway and the hate is growing too.

It's not like America was super popular before all of this, it was more tolerated then celebrated. This very large straw broke the camel's back and everyone is working on moving away and after that's done, why come back?

在 HN 中查看
jjk166 2026/04/07 08:29
美国之于军事强国就像阿根廷之于经济体一样，未来的前景并不光明。
America will be to military powers what Argentina is to economies is not exactly a bright prospect for the future.
在 HN 中查看
slyall 2026/04/07 07:42
“与你的生活保持一致”和“相信你的生活”之间是有区别的
There is a difference between "aligning with" and "trusting with your life"
在 HN 中查看
JumpCrisscross 2026/04/07 07:49
> “与你的生活保持一致”和“相信你的生活”之间是有区别的

几乎？在密切相关的背景下？我认为地缘政治中不存在这种划分。

美国核保护伞下的每一个盟友都信任华盛顿特区的生命，北约、AUKUS 和其他防御伙伴关系也是如此。

> There is a difference between "aligning with" and "trusting with your life"

Practically? In a germane context? I don't think that delineation exists in geopolitics.

Every U.S. ally under its nuclear umbrella trusts its life with D.C. Same with NATO and AUKUS and other defensive partnerships.

在 HN 中查看
MarkusQ 2026/04/07 07:38
所以故事是……一个反对现在执政党的刊物，引用了一些现在已经下台的政党的人的话，说他们下台了真的很糟糕，我们可能永远都无法恢复了？

这与我们角色互换时发出的抱怨有何不同？

我知道你们彼此憎恨，但如果你们中的任何一个人能谈论一些事情，而不是把它变成抱怨自己的一方是多么伟大、高贵和善良，而另一方是多么糟糕，那就太好了。

So the story is... a publication that opposes the party currently in power, quoting a few people from the side that's presently out of power, saying that their being out of power is really bad, and we may never recover?

How is this different than the whining we get when the roles are reversed?

I realize you folks hate each other, but it would be nice if either of you could talk about something without turning it into a rant about how great, noble and good your side is and how awful the other side is.

在 HN 中查看
AnimalMuppet 2026/04/07 09:56
对于中立的人来说（是的，幽默一下），特朗普政府在摧毁美国声誉方面所做的努力比我一生中的任何其他政府都多得多（好吧，也许是尼克松——我对他的第一手资料不太记得了）。

但我还要说，拜登虽然没有特朗普那么糟糕，但比尼克松以来的任何人都更糟糕。

To someone neutral (yeah, humor me), the Trump administration has done far more to demolish the reputation of the US than any other administration in my lifetime (OK, maybe Nixon - I don't remember all that much about him firsthand).

But I would also say that Biden, while not as bad as Trump, was worse than anybody since Nixon.

在 HN 中查看
jjtwixman 2026/04/07 14:53
我喜欢虚假和荒谬的“中立”。
I love fake and nonsensical “neutrality”.
在 HN 中查看
ggm 2026/04/07 07:18
承认它可以变，就承认了打蜡和变的概念，这就承认了打蜡的概念。它可能会再次上涨。这取决于其他选择利用其新获得的国际声誉变得多么糟糕。

而且，它是建立在有用的慷慨之上的。我认为对我来说，终结的开始（我确信它早于这一切，但这是我更加意识到这一点的时候）是当联合国的资金枯竭时，因为激进的美国基督教讨厌妇女的生育权利。这是对改善非洲健康状况的理性态度的巨大转变，目的是什么？用于短期国内议程。联合国系统性腐败和洗钱是一个大问题，但推动这一变革的并不是“清理联合国”，而是将避孕药放回盒子里。

[编辑：“本世纪”的意思是“过去25年”，因为在越南时代，美国在世界范围内的声誉相当低。我总是忘记我们正处于一个新世纪。对性的战争是准总统南希·里根时代的事情。]

Admitting it can wain admits the concepts of waxing and waining, which admits the concept of waxing. It could rise again. it depends how bad the other choices become exploiting their new found international reputation.

Also, it was built on useful largesse. I think the beginning of the end to me (I am sure it predates this, but this is when I became more conscious of it) was when the funding of the UN dried up because militant american christianity hates women's reproductive rights. That was a massive flip in posture towards a rational approach to improved health in Africa and for what? For a short term domestic agenda. The UN systematic corruption and money laundering was a huge issue but what motivated the change wasn't "cleaning up the UN" it was putting contraception back in the box.

[edit: "this century" meaning "in the last 25 years" because during the Vietnam era, American reputation was pretty low worldwide. I keep forgetting we're in a new century. The war on sex was President-pro-tem Nancy Reagan era stuff.]

在 HN 中查看
tkel 2026/04/07 07:50
人们常常将“声誉”置于其他事物之上，就好像它在政治上是可行的或有形的。 事实并非如此，它是人们个人感情对民族国家行为的投射。 老实说，这是奇怪的行为。如此强烈地认同一个民族国家，以至于关心它的“声誉”而不是实际的物质措施。这是一种准社会现象，表明人们将政治视为一种消费娱乐形式，而不是他们日常生活中参与的事情。就好像您是一名外国外交官一样，这可能是人们谈论“声誉”的唯一重要时刻。
People often prioritize "reputation" over other things, as if it is politically actionable or tangible. It's not, and it's a projection of peoples' personal feelings onto the actions of a nation-state. Honestly, it's odd behavior. To identify with a nation-state so strongly to care about it's "reputation" over actual material measures. It's parasocial and indicitave of people treating politics as a consumer form of entertainment, and not something they engage in in their daily lives. As if you were a foreign diplomat, might be the only time "reputation" mattered in the way that people talk about it.
在 HN 中查看
AnimalMuppet 2026/04/07 10:03
在国际上，声誉本质上是你的国家在其他国家的思维中的表现。 这是他们对你未来行为方式的期望值。

人们优先考虑声誉，因为这几乎就是一切。 条约？ 当然可以，但是该国遵守其条款的可能性有多大？ 协议？ 同样的问题。 投资地点？ 那里的法治有多好？这种状况持续下去的可能性有多大？ 这些都是声誉问题；也就是说，它们是关于过去行为预测的未来行为的问题。

Internationally, reputation is, essentially, your country's track record projected forward in other nations' thinking. It's their expectation value for how you will behave in the future.

People prioritize reputation because that's pretty much all there is to go on. Treaties? Sure, but how likely is the country to keep the terms of it? Agreements? Same question. Place for investments? How good is the rule of law there, and how likely is that to continue? Those are reputation questions; that is, they are questions about future behavior as predicted by past behavior.

在 HN 中查看
